title: Temporal Workflow Extension
description: Agent sessions are orchestrated as durable Temporal workflows. A singleton SessionManagerWorkflow acts as the orchestrator for all agent session lifecycles, while individual AgentWorkflow instances handle conversational turns.
actors: TUI Client (temporal_sandbox_tui.py) — human operator driving sessions interactively
Developer — starts Temporal worker and server, configures backends
Temporal Worker — executes workflow and activity code
AgentWorkflow — individual long-lived agent session workflow
OpenAI Agents SDK (Runner) — processes each conversation turn
use_cases: Create new agent session (CreateSessionRequest: cwd, manifest, backend)
List all active sessions (query, sorted newest first)
Rename an existing session (RenameRequest: workflow_id, title)
Fork a session to same or different backend (ForkSessionRequest: source_workflow_id, title, target_backend)
Switch session backend in-place (SwitchBackendRequest: source_workflow_id, target_backend)
Pause an agent session (stops active sandbox session)
Resume a paused/forked session with prior conversation history
Send user message to agent (chat turn)
Approve or reject agent tool calls (human-in-the-loop)
Query live turn state (polling: status, tool calls, approval requests)
Query workflow snapshot (WorkflowSnapshot: history, sandbox_session_state, snapshot)
Update session snapshot ID (after backend switch/fork)

updates: create_session(CreateSessionRequest) -> str  [starts child AgentWorkflow, registers SessionInfo]
fork_session(ForkSessionRequest) -> str  [pause source, snapshot, start new child]
switch_backend(SwitchBackendRequest) -> str  [signals AgentWorkflow to change backend in-place]
destroy_session(workflow_id: str) -> None  [signals AgentWorkflow.destroy, removes from registry]
queries: list_sessions() -> list[SessionInfo]  [returns all active sessions, newest first]

AgentWorkflow.behavior: Idles between conversation turns; each turn calls Runner.run() with SandboxRunConfig targeting active backend

Activities: pause_workflow(workflow_id: str) -> None  [executes AgentWorkflow.pause update via client]

data_models: SessionInfo: workflow_id, title, created_at, cwd, backend: BackendConfig, parent_workflow_id, fork_count, snapshot_id
CreateSessionRequest: cwd, manifest: Manifest|None, backend: BackendConfig
WorkflowSnapshot: sandbox_session_state, snapshot: SnapshotBase|None, previous_response_id, history: list
AgentRequest: messages, cwd, backend, sandbox_session_state, snapshot, previous_response_id, history, manifest
key_relationships: TUI Client --update--> SessionManagerWorkflow: create_session, fork_session, switch_backend, destroy_session
SessionManagerWorkflow --start_child_workflow--> AgentWorkflow: on create_session and fork_session

create_session: SessionManagerWorkflow: derive manifest from backend config if not provided
SessionManagerWorkflow: start_child_workflow(AgentWorkflow.run, AgentRequest, id=workflow_id, ABANDON policy)
fork_session: SessionManagerWorkflow: determine target_backend and fork title
SessionManagerWorkflow: start_child_workflow(AgentWorkflow.run, AgentRequest with history+snapshot, ABANDON)
Register new SessionInfo with parent_workflow_id set

important_notes: SessionManagerWorkflow is a singleton (well-known ID 'session-manager'), lives forever until shutdown signal
AgentWorkflow instances are long-lived, idle between turns, persist indefinitely in Temporal
ParentClosePolicy.ABANDON ensures child agent workflows survive if manager restarts
Fork uses portable SnapshotBase (cross-backend) but NOT backend-specific session state
Switch backend is in-place: same workflow_id, clears session state, next turn creates fresh backend session
All Temporal client connections: localhost:7233 with pydantic_data_converter
Backends supported: Daytona, Docker, E2B, Local (unix)
OpenAIAgentsPlugin used for tracing context during session creation
